expected_results: The three monitor commands exist (run_close_monitor, run_outcome_router, dispatch_notifications) and the new run_monitor_chain orchestrates them in dependency order without adding execution logic.
The scheduled chain is idempotent: repeated per-minute runs create no duplicate TradeOutcomeRecord / NotificationCandidate rows.
The chain is safe when there is no work: on an empty DB it runs cleanly, reports all-zero counts, and exits 0.
The chain cannot create an order: a full pass over a closed WIN trade creates zero ExecutionJob rows (no order_send / order_check / bridge call anywhere reachable).

The chain cannot send a Telegram message: dispatch is a no-op when NOTIFICATION_DISPATCH_ENABLED is off (default), and even when enabled the only transport is dry-run (NotificationDelivery.transmitted always False).
The chain cannot publish to WIMS: no ConsumptionContract is created; the command imports no wims surface (static AST).
The chain only processes pre-existing closed trades / outcome records / candidates and creates internal records only.
Logs are visible: one grep-friendly summary line per run to /var/log/guvfx/monitor_chain.log, echoing the dispatch posture.
Rollback is simple: install_monitor_cron.sh --remove deletes only the marked cron line; the existing scheduler crons and all containers are untouched; nothing is deployed by merging.
No model/migration change; full backend suite green; make governance-check + secret-scan pass.
actual_results: MonitorChainSafetyTests: empty-DB no-op (zeros, exit 0) PASS; a WIN-trade pass creates 0 ExecutionJob + 0 ConsumptionContract + 0 NotificationDelivery PASS; dispatch no-op when flag OFF (candidate stays PENDING, 0 deliveries) PASS; dispatch transmits nothing even when NOTIFICATION_DISPATCH_ENABLED=true (candidate SENT but NotificationDelivery.transmitted=False) PASS.

MonitorChainPipelineTests: single pass flows close->outcome in order (candidate exists => outcome existed => close ran first) PASS; LOSS makes an outcome but no candidate PASS; chain idempotent across two passes (1 record, 1 candidate) PASS.

MonitorChainResilienceTests: a raising close_monitor step is logged (STEP FAILED) and the outcome-router + dispatch still run (failures=close_monitor in summary) PASS; --fail-fast re-raises as CommandError PASS.

MonitorChainStaticBoundaryTests: the command module references none of order_send/order_check/MetaTrader5/mt5/requests/httpx/urllib/create_place_order_job/create_open_trade_job/agent_order/ConsumptionContract/wims/deliver_trade_result PASS.
run_monitor_chain --limit 0: structural no-op confirmed (slice [:0]) — full wiring runs, zero rows touched, clean summary line, dispatch enabled=False.
Full backend suite: Ran 466 tests OK (456 pre-packet + 10 monitor-chain).
makemigrations --check execution: No changes detected (no model change). bash -n on both shell scripts OK. make governance-check OK; secret-scan OK; EVIDENCE-LINT PASS.

Adversarial 4-lens scheduling-boundary review (order-boundary / transmit-wims-boundary / idempotency-safety / ops-correctness, 5 agents, ~264k tokens) + opus synthesis: decision GO, must_fix=[]. Three of four lenses clean; ops-correctness raised 3 items all demoted to optional nits. Applied hardening: end-anchored the install-cron marker (grep -E "# guvfx-monitor-chain$") so a future sibling line (e.g. -v2) can never be collaterally removed by --remove (verified: only the exact managed line matches); clarified the verify-script --limit 0 and transmitted-assertion comments. --limit-0-comment nit was dropped by synthesis as accurate.
limitations: Repo-only. Merging deploys NOTHING — the cron is installed by running deploy/monitor-scheduler/install_monitor_cron.sh on the prod host, a separate operator action. No E3 enablement, no provider arming, no order placement, no notification transmission, no auto-live. E3 remains RED.

The verify_monitor_chain.sh boundary assertions and install idempotency were validated by design + local test-DB exercise + bash -n; they were NOT run against the live prod estate in this packet (no deployment).

run_monitor_chain adds no execution logic — its safety is inherited from the three already-shipped, separately-tested functions (close_monitor / outcome_router / dispatcher); this packet re-verifies the composed chain, not the individual functions' internals.

With no active demo outcomes today the scheduled chain is effectively a no-op; once E3 is later enabled it begins classifying real closed demo trades (still internal + dry-run only until the notification transport is separately, explicitly enabled under its own gated packet).

verify_monitor_chain.sh's transmitted-count check is a NEGATIVE assertion valid for the only transport that exists (TelegramDryRunTransport always records transmitted=False and opens no socket). If a real Telegram transport is ever added, this script must be re-verified to assert against that transport's own audit trail before being trusted (noted in-script; flagged by the review).
